english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Hebrews

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic enim Melchisedech, rex Salem, sacerdos Dei summi, qui obviavit Abrahæ regresso a cæde regum, et benedixit ei:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος γὰρ ὁ μελχισέδεκ βασιλεὺς σαλήμ ἱερεὺς τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ὑψίστου ὁ συναντήσας ἀβραὰμ ὑποστρέφοντι ἀπὸ τῆς κοπῆς τῶν βασιλέων καὶ εὐλογήσας αὐτόν
verses.king_james: For this Melchisedec, king of Salem, priest of the most high God, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him;

verses.latin_vulgate: cui et decimas omnium divisit Abraham: primum quidem qui interpretatur rex justitiæ: deinde autem et rex Salem, quod est, rex pacis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ᾧ καὶ δεκάτην ἀπὸ πάντων ἐμέρισεν ἀβραάμ πρῶτον μὲν ἑρμηνευόμενος βασιλεὺς δικαιοσύνης ἔπειτα δὲ καὶ βασιλεὺς σαλήμ ὅ ἐστιν βασιλεὺς εἰρήνης
verses.king_james: To whom also Abraham gave a tenth part of all; first being by interpretation King of righteousness, and after that also King of Salem, which is, King of peace;

verses.latin_vulgate: sine patre, sine matre, sine genealogia, neque initium dierum, neque finem vitæ habens, assimilatus autem Filio Dei, manet sacerdos in perpetuum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπάτωρ ἀμήτωρ ἀγενεαλόγητος μήτε ἀρχὴν ἡμερῶν μήτε ζωῆς τέλος ἔχων ἀφωμοιωμένος δὲ τῷ υἱῷ τοῦ θεοῦ μένει ἱερεὺς εἰς τὸ διηνεκές
verses.king_james: Without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days, nor end of life; but made like unto the Son of God; abideth a priest continually.

verses.latin_vulgate: Intuemini autem quantus sit hic, cui et decimas dedit de præcipuis Abraham patriarcha.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θεωρεῖτε δὲ πηλίκος οὗτος ᾧ καὶ δεκάτην ἀβραὰμ ἔδωκεν ἐκ τῶν ἀκροθινίων ὁ πατριάρχης
verses.king_james: Now consider how great this man *was*, unto whom even the patriarch Abraham gave the tenth of the spoils.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quidem de filiis Levi sacerdotium accipientes, mandatum habent decimas sumere a populo secundum legem, id est, a fratribus suis: quamquam et ipsi exierint de lumbis Abrahæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ μὲν ἐκ τῶν υἱῶν λευὶ τὴν ἱερατείαν λαμβάνοντες ἐντολὴν ἔχουσιν ἀποδεκατοῦν τὸν λαὸν κατὰ τὸν νόμον τουτέστιν τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς αὐτῶν καίπερ ἐξεληλυθότας ἐκ τῆς ὀσφύος ἀβραάμ
verses.king_james: And verily they that are of the sons of Levi, who receive the office of the priesthood, have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brethren, though they come out of the loins of Abraham:

verses.latin_vulgate: Cujus autem generatio non annumeratur in eis, decimas sumpsit ab Abraham, et hunc, qui habebat repromissiones, benedixit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ μὴ γενεαλογούμενος ἐξ αὐτῶν δεδεκάτωκεν τὸν ἀβραάμ καὶ τὸν ἔχοντα τὰς ἐπαγγελίας εὐλόγηκεν
verses.king_james: But he whose descent is not counted from them received tithes of Abraham, and blessed him that had the promises.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sine ulla autem contradictione, quod minus est, a meliore benedicitur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χωρὶς δὲ πάσης ἀντιλογίας τὸ ἔλαττον ὑπὸ τοῦ κρείττονος εὐλογεῖται
verses.king_james: And without all contradiction the less is blessed of the better.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hic quidem, decimas morientes homines accipiunt: ibi autem contestatur, quia vivit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὧδε μὲν δεκάτας ἀποθνῄσκοντες ἄνθρωποι λαμβάνουσιν ἐκεῖ δὲ μαρτυρούμενος ὅτι ζῇ
verses.king_james: And here men that die receive tithes; but there he *receiveth them*, of whom it is witnessed that he liveth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et (ut ita dictum sit) per Abraham, et Levi, qui decimas accepit, decimatus est:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὡς ἔπος εἰπεῖν διὰ ἀβραὰμ καὶ λευὶ ὁ δεκάτας λαμβάνων δεδεκάτωται
verses.king_james: And as I may so say, Levi also, who receiveth tithes, payed tithes in Abraham.

verses.latin_vulgate: adhuc enim in lumbis patris erat, quando obviavit ei Melchisedech.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔτι γὰρ ἐν τῇ ὀσφύϊ τοῦ πατρὸς ἦν ὅτε συνήντησεν αὐτῷ ὁ μελχισέδεκ
verses.king_james: For he was yet in the loins of his father, when Melchisedec met him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ergo consummatio per sacerdotium Leviticum erat (populus enim sub ipso legem accepit) quid adhuc necessarium fuit secundum ordinem Melchisedech, alium surgere sacerdotem, et non secundum ordinem Aaron dici?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ μὲν οὖν τελείωσις διὰ τῆς λευιτικῆς ἱερωσύνης ἦν ὁ λαὸς γὰρ ἐπ᾽ αὐτῇ νενομοθέτητο, τίς ἔτι χρεία κατὰ τὴν τάξιν μελχισέδεκ ἕτερον ἀνίστασθαι ἱερέα καὶ οὐ κατὰ τὴν τάξιν ἀαρὼν λέγεσθαι
verses.king_james: If therefore perfection were by the Levitical priesthood, (for under it the people received the law,) what further need *was there* that another priest should rise after the order of Melchisedec, and not be called after the order of Aaron?

verses.latin_vulgate: Translato enim sacerdotio, necesse erat ut et legis translatio fiat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετατιθεμένης γὰρ τῆς ἱερωσύνης ἐξ ἀνάγκης καὶ νόμου μετάθεσις γίνεται
verses.king_james: For the priesthood being changed, there is made of necessity a change also of the law.

verses.latin_vulgate: In quo enim hæc dicuntur, de alia tribu est, de qua nullus altari præsto fuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐφ᾽ ὃν γὰρ λέγεται ταῦτα φυλῆς ἑτέρας μετέσχηκεν ἀφ᾽ ἡς οὐδεὶς προσέσχηκεν τῷ θυσιαστηρίῳ
verses.king_james: For he of whom these things are spoken pertaineth to another tribe, of which no man gave attendance at the altar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Manifestum est enim quod ex Juda ortus sit Dominus noster: in qua tribu nihil de sacerdotibus Moyses locutus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρόδηλον γὰρ ὅτι ἐξ ἰούδα ἀνατέταλκεν ὁ κύριος ἡμῶν εἰς ἣν φυλὴν οὐδὲν περὶ ἱερωσυνής μωσῆς ἐλάλησεν
verses.king_james: For *it is* evident that our Lord sprang out of Juda; of which tribe Moses spake nothing concerning priesthood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et amplius adhuc manifestum est: si secundum similitudinem Melchisedech exsurgat alius sacerdos,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ περισσότερον ἔτι κατάδηλόν ἐστιν εἰ κατὰ τὴν ὁμοιότητα μελχισέδεκ ἀνίσταται ἱερεὺς ἕτερος
verses.king_james: And it is yet far more evident: for that after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui non secundum legem mandati carnalis factus est, sed secundum virtutem vitæ insolubilis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς οὐ κατὰ νόμον ἐντολῆς σαρκίκης γέγονεν ἀλλὰ κατὰ δύναμιν ζωῆς ἀκαταλύτου
verses.king_james: Who is made, not after the law of a carnal commandment, but after the power of an endless life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Contestatur enim: Quoniam tu es sacerdos in æternum, secundum ordinem Melchisedech.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μαρτυρεῖ γὰρ ὅτι σὺ ἱερεὺς εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα κατὰ τὴν τάξιν μελχισέδεκ
verses.king_james: For he testifieth, Thou *art* a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reprobatio quidem fit præcedentis mandati, propter infirmitatem ejus, et inutilitatem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀθέτησις μὲν γὰρ γίνεται προαγούσης ἐντολῆς διὰ τὸ αὐτῆς ἀσθενὲς καὶ ἀνωφελές
verses.king_james: For there is verily a disannulling of the commandment going before for the weakness and unprofitableness thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: nihil enim ad perfectum adduxit lex: introductio vero melioris spei, per quam proximamus ad Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲν γὰρ ἐτελείωσεν ὁ νόμος ἐπεισαγωγὴ δὲ κρείττονος ἐλπίδος δι᾽ ἡς ἐγγίζομεν τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: For the law made nothing perfect, but the bringing in of a better hope *did*; by the which we draw nigh unto God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quantum est non sine jurejurando (alii quidem sine jurejurando sacerdotes facti sunt,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ καθ᾽ ὅσον οὐ χωρὶς ὁρκωμοσίας οἵ μέν γάρ χωρίς ὁρκωμοσίας εἰσιν ἱερεῖς γεγονότες
verses.king_james: And inasmuch as not without an oath *he was made priest*:

verses.latin_vulgate: hic autem cum jurejurando per eum, qui dixit ad illum: Juravit Dominus, et non pœnitebit eum: tu es sacerdos in æternum):
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ μετὰ ὁρκωμοσίας, διὰ τοῦ λέγοντος πρὸς αὐτόν ὢμοσεν κύριος καὶ οὐ μεταμεληθήσεται σὺ ἱερεὺς εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα κατὰ τὴν τάξιν μελχισεδέκ
verses.king_james: (For those priests were made without an oath; but this with an oath by him that said unto him, The Lord sware and will not repent, Thou *art* a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec:)

verses.latin_vulgate: in tantum melioris testamenti sponsor factus est Jesus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατὰ τοσοῦτον κρείττονος διαθήκης γέγονεν ἔγγυος ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: By so much was Jesus made a surety of a better testament.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et alii quidem plures facti sunt sacerdotes, idcirco quod morte prohiberentur permanere:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ μὲν πλείονές εἰσιν γεγονότες ἱερεῖς διὰ τὸ θανάτῳ κωλύεσθαι παραμένειν
verses.king_james: And they truly were many priests, because they were not suffered to continue by reason of death:

verses.latin_vulgate: hic autem eo quod maneat in æternum, sempiternum habet sacerdotium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ διὰ τὸ μένειν αὐτὸν εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα ἀπαράβατον ἔχει τὴν ἱερωσύνην
verses.king_james: But this *man*, because he continueth ever, hath an unchangeable priesthood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unde et salvare in perpetuum potest accedentes per semetipsum ad Deum: semper vivens ad interpellandum pro nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅθεν καὶ σῴζειν εἰς τὸ παντελὲς δύναται τοὺς προσερχομένους δι᾽ αὐτοῦ τῷ θεῷ πάντοτε ζῶν εἰς τὸ ἐντυγχάνειν ὑπὲρ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Wherefore he is able also to save them to the uttermost that come unto God by him, seeing he ever liveth to make intercession for them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Talis enim decebat ut nobis esset pontifex, sanctus, innocens, impollutus, segregatus a peccatoribus, et excelsior cælis factus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοιοῦτος γὰρ ἡμῖν ἔπρεπεν ἀρχιερεύς ὅσιος ἄκακος ἀμίαντος κεχωρισμένος ἀπὸ τῶν ἁμαρτωλῶν καὶ ὑψηλότερος τῶν οὐρανῶν γενόμενος
verses.king_james: For such an high priest became us, *who is* holy, harmless, undefiled, separate from sinners, and made higher than the heavens;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui non habet necessitatem quotidie, quemadmodum sacerdotes, prius pro suis delictis hostias offerre, deinde pro populi: hoc enim fecit semel, seipsum offerendo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς οὐκ ἔχει καθ᾽ ἡμέραν ἀνάγκην ὥσπερ οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς πρότερον ὑπὲρ τῶν ἰδίων ἁμαρτιῶν θυσίας ἀναφέρειν ἔπειτα τῶν τοῦ λαοῦ τοῦτο γὰρ ἐποίησεν ἐφάπαξ ἑαυτὸν ἀνενέγκας
verses.king_james: Who needeth not daily, as those high priests, to offer up sacrifice, first for his own sins, and then for the people's: for this he did once, when he offered up himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lex enim homines constituit sacerdotes infirmitatem habentes: sermo autem jurisjurandi, qui post legem est, Filium in æternum perfectum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ νόμος γὰρ ἀνθρώπους καθίστησιν ἀρχιερεῖς ἔχοντας ἀσθένειαν ὁ λόγος δὲ τῆς ὁρκωμοσίας τῆς μετὰ τὸν νόμον υἱὸν εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα τετελειωμένον
verses.king_james: For the law maketh men high priests which have infirmity; but the word of the oath, which was since the law, *maketh* the Son, who is consecrated for evermore.
